edges.description: Reduced CBP/p300-dependent histone acetylation impairs neurodevelopmental transcriptional programs, contributing to intellectual disability.
edges.source: CBP/p300 Haploinsufficiency and Loss of Histone Acetyltransferase Activity

edges.description: Loss of one functional CBP/p300 copy, which have tumor-suppressor roles, is proposed to underlie the increased incidence of certain benign tumors in RSTS.
edges.source: CBP/p300 Haploinsufficiency and Loss of Histone Acetyltransferase Activity

nodes.description: Broad great toes (halluces), often angulated, are a hallmark feature.

evidence.snippet: broad and often angulated thumbs and halluces

evidence.reference_title: Rubinstein-Taybi syndrome caused by mutations in the transcriptional co-activator CBP.
evidence.snippet: broad thumbs, broad big toes

nodes.description: Broad and often angulated thumbs are a hallmark feature of RSTS.

evidence.reference_title: Rubinstein-Taybi syndrome caused by mutations in the transcriptional co-activator CBP.
evidence.snippet: a well-defined syndrome with facial abnormalities, broad thumbs, broad big toes and mental retardation as the main clinical features
broad and often angulated thumbs and halluces

nodes.description: Heterozygous loss-of-function variants or deletions in CREBBP or EP300 reduce the dosage of the paralogous histone acetyltransferases CBP and p300. Both proteins function as transcriptional coactivators that acetylate histones and other substrates; a certain level of CBP is essential for normal development. Loss of one functional allele lowers histone acetyltransferase activity, producing aberrant chromatin regulation and dysregulated transcription that drives the developmental phenotype.
nodes.id: CBP/p300 Haploinsufficiency and Loss of Histone Acetyltransferase Activity

evidence.reference_title: Genetic heterogeneity in Rubinstein-Taybi syndrome: mutations in both the CBP and EP300 genes cause disease.
evidence.snippet: There is a direct link between loss of acetyl transferase activity and RSTS, which indicates that the disorder is caused by aberrant chromatin regulation.

evidence.reference_title: Genetic heterogeneity in Rubinstein-Taybi syndrome: mutations in both the CBP and EP300 genes cause disease.
evidence.snippet: CREB-binding protein and p300 function as transcriptional coactivators in the regulation of gene expression through various signal-transduction pathways. Both are potent histone acetyl transferases.

evidence.reference_title: Rubinstein-Taybi syndrome caused by mutations in the transcriptional co-activator CBP.
evidence.snippet: the gene for the human CREB binding protein (CBP), a nuclear protein participating as a co-activator in cyclic-AMP-regulated gene expression

evidence.reference_title: Diagnosis and management in Rubinstein-Taybi syndrome: first international consensus statement.
evidence.snippet: It is caused by variants in either of two genes (CREBBP, EP300) which encode for the proteins CBP and p300, which both have a function in transcription regulation and histone acetylation.

nodes.description: Congenital heart defects are among the additional features of RSTS.

evidence.snippet: Additional features include ocular abnormalities, hearing loss, respiratory difficulties, congenital heart defects, renal abnormalities, cryptorchidism, feeding problems, recurrent infections, and severe constipation.

nodes.description: Cryptorchidism (undescended testes) is reported among additional features.

evidence.snippet: cryptorchidism, feeding problems, recurrent infections, and severe constipation

nodes.description: Characteristic craniofacial features include downslanted palpebral fissures, low-hanging columella, high palate, grimacing smile, and talon cusps.

evidence.snippet: Characteristic craniofacial features include downslanted palpebral fissures, low-hanging columella, high palate, grimacing smile, and talon cusps.

nodes.description: Feeding problems are reported among additional features of RSTS, with monitoring of feeding recommended especially in the first year of life.

evidence.snippet: cryptorchidism, feeding problems, recurrent infections, and severe constipation

nodes.description: Hearing loss is reported among additional features of RSTS.

evidence.snippet: Additional features include ocular abnormalities, hearing loss, respiratory difficulties

nodes.description: A high-arched palate is a characteristic craniofacial feature.

evidence.snippet: Characteristic craniofacial features include downslanted palpebral fissures, low-hanging columella, high palate, grimacing smile, and talon cusps.

nodes.description: CBP/p300-dependent histone acetylation is required for the transcriptional programs underlying neurogenesis, neuronal differentiation, and activity-dependent gene expression involved in learning and memory. Reduced acetyltransferase activity impairs these programs, contributing to the global developmental delay and moderate-to-severe intellectual disability characteristic of RSTS.

evidence.snippet: Rubinstein-Taybi syndrome (RSTS) is characterized by distinctive facial features, broad and often angulated thumbs and halluces, short stature, and moderate-to-severe intellectual disability.

nodes.description: Moderate-to-severe intellectual disability is characteristic, with average IQ between 35 and 50, though developmental outcome varies; some individuals with EP300-related RSTS have normal intellect.

evidence.snippet: Average IQ ranges between 35 and 50; however, developmental outcome varies considerably. Some individuals with EP300-related RSTS have normal intellect.

nodes.description: Meningiomas are the most frequent benign tumor in RSTS, with significantly elevated incidence compared with the general population.

evidence.reference_title: Benign and malignant tumors in Rubinstein-Taybi syndrome.
evidence.snippet: Meningiomas and pilomatricomas were the most frequent benign tumors and their incidence was significantly elevated in comparison to the general Dutch population.

nodes.description: Individuals with RSTS show a high prevalence of neuropsychiatric and behavioral challenges across the lifespan, including OCD-like symptoms, anxiety, and challenging behaviors. RSTS2 (EP300) individuals tend to have better adaptive behavior but higher social phobia than RSTS1 (CREBBP).

evidence.reference_title: Behavioral and neuropsychiatric challenges across the lifespan in individuals with Rubinstein-Taybi syndrome.
evidence.snippet: Results revealed a high prevalence of neuropsychiatric and behavioral challenges across ages.

evidence.reference_title: Behavioral and neuropsychiatric challenges across the lifespan in individuals with Rubinstein-Taybi syndrome.
evidence.snippet: individuals with RSTS2 had better adaptive behavior and living skills and less stereotypic behaviors but higher social phobia than individuals with RSTS1

nodes.description: Obesity may develop in childhood or adolescence.

evidence.snippet: Obesity may develop in childhood or adolescence.

nodes.description: Ocular abnormalities are reported among additional features of RSTS, and annual eye evaluations are recommended in surveillance.

evidence.snippet: Additional features include ocular abnormalities, hearing loss, respiratory difficulties

nodes.description: Pilomatricomas (benign skin adnexal tumors of hair matrix origin) are, alongside meningiomas, the most frequent benign tumor in RSTS, with significantly elevated incidence compared with the general population.

evidence.reference_title: Benign and malignant tumors in Rubinstein-Taybi syndrome.
evidence.snippet: Meningiomas and pilomatricomas were the most frequent benign tumors and their incidence was significantly elevated in comparison to the general Dutch population.

nodes.description: Head circumference is often normal at birth, then drops in percentile over the first months of life, resulting in postnatal microcephaly.

evidence.snippet: height, weight, and head circumference percentiles rapidly drop in the first few months of life

nodes.description: Manifestations of immune dysfunction, affecting mostly B cells, are more common than previously recognized in RSTS. Antibody defects (including hypogammaglobulinemia with low B cell counts and reduced switched memory B cells) occur in a subset of patients, and syndromic immunodeficiency is frequently diagnosed.

evidence.reference_title: Prevalence of Immunological Defects in a Cohort of 97 Rubinstein-Taybi Syndrome Patients.
evidence.snippet: antibody defects were observed in 11.3% of subjects. In particular, these patients presented hypogammaglobulinemia associated with low B cell counts and reduction of switched memory B cell numbers.

evidence.reference_title: Prevalence of Immunological Defects in a Cohort of 97 Rubinstein-Taybi Syndrome Patients.
evidence.snippet: Syndromic immunodeficiency was diagnosed in 46.4% of individuals.

nodes.description: Recurrent infections are reported among additional features of RSTS.

evidence.snippet: cryptorchidism, feeding problems, recurrent infections, and severe constipation

evidence.reference_title: Prevalence of Immunological Defects in a Cohort of 97 Rubinstein-Taybi Syndrome Patients.
evidence.snippet: Recurrent or severe infections, autoimmune/autoinflammatory complications, and lymphoproliferation were observed in 72.1%, 12.3%, and 8.2% of patients.

nodes.description: Renal abnormalities are reported among additional features of RSTS, with routine monitoring for renal anomalies recommended.

evidence.snippet: congenital heart defects, renal abnormalities, cryptorchidism

nodes.description: Respiratory difficulties are reported among additional features of RSTS.

evidence.snippet: Additional features include ocular abnormalities, hearing loss, respiratory difficulties

nodes.description: Severe constipation is reported among additional features.

evidence.snippet: cryptorchidism, feeding problems, recurrent infections, and severe constipation

nodes.description: Prenatal growth is often normal, then height, weight, and head circumference percentiles rapidly drop in the first months of life; short stature is typical in adulthood.

evidence.snippet: Prenatal growth is often normal, then height, weight, and head circumference percentiles rapidly drop in the first few months of life. Short stature is typical in adulthood.

nodes.description: Talon cusps (accessory cusp-like structures on teeth) are a characteristic dental finding in RSTS.

evidence.snippet: downslanted palpebral fissures, low-hanging columella, high palate, grimacing smile, and talon cusps

nodes.description: CBP and p300 have tumor-suppressor roles, and loss of one functional copy is associated with an increased incidence of certain benign tumors, particularly meningiomas and pilomatricomas. Petrij et al. proposed that loss of one functional CBP allele may underlie the propensity for malignancy; population-based data confirm a significantly elevated incidence of meningiomas and pilomatricomas in RSTS.

evidence.reference_title: Benign and malignant tumors in Rubinstein-Taybi syndrome.
evidence.snippet: Meningiomas and pilomatricomas were the most frequent benign tumors and their incidence was significantly elevated in comparison to the general Dutch population.

evidence.reference_title: Rubinstein-Taybi syndrome caused by mutations in the transcriptional co-activator CBP.
evidence.snippet: the loss of one functional copy of the CBP gene underlies the developmental abnormalities in RTS and possibly the propensity for malignancy
